per_outcome.operationalization: Count of successfully resolved chats per agent per hour, measured from platform logs
Mean duration in minutes from chat start to completion per agent, measured from platform timestamps
Count of chats handled per agent per hour, accounting for concurrent multitasking, from platform logs
Percentage of chats marked as successfully resolved out of total chats handled by agent

Customer satisfaction metric: percentage who would recommend minus percentage who would not, from post-chat surveys. This measures customer satisfaction, not the quality of the technical solution or agent output.
Rubric score 1-5 rating how cogent and understandable the agent's text is, evaluation method not specified
Binary or scaled assessment of whether agent text resembles native American English, measurement method not specified
Sentiment score -1 to 1 measuring customer emotional tone, measurement method not specified. This captures customer emotional state, not output quality.
Sentiment score -1 to 1 measuring agent emotional tone, measurement method not specified. This captures agent well-being/affect, not output quality.
Percentage of chats where customer requests manager/supervisor, aggregated monthly. This measures customer dissatisfaction/escalation behavior, not output quality per se.
Percentage of workers who leave employment, particularly among newer workers. This measures retention/turnover, not output quality.
Cosine similarity 0-1 comparing language patterns between high-skill and low-skill workers. This measures skill convergence, not output quality or speed.
